summary: Fixed HTTP 200 with 'No models provided' response text. Provisioner had no fallback defaults for model/provider after HERMES_DEFAULT_MODEL/PROVIDER were deleted from Railway. Made provisioner fall back to OPENROUTER_MODEL env var or 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash' with provider='openrouter'. All 19 tests pass, E2E verified.

narrative: Provisioner line 19: changed defaults to fall back to serverEnv.OPENROUTER_MODEL then 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash' for model, and 'openrouter' for provider
index.js line 141: added OPENROUTER_MODEL to serverEnv passed to provisioner

debuggingNarrative.bug: Session returns 200 gateway:running, message returns 200 but output says 'HTTP 400: No models provided'. Hermes gateway is running but has no model configured.

whatWeTried.1. Check Railway env: HERMES_DEFAULT_MODEL and HERMES_DEFAULT_PROVIDER were deleted during the gateway crash fix. They no longer exist in Railway variables.
whatWeTried.2. Check provisioner output: Provisioner writes model/provider to config.yaml only if truthy. With undefined values, these lines were omitted → Hermes had zero models.
whatWeTried.3. Try explicit model in body: Specifying model in request body returns same error. Hermes needs model configured in config.yaml AND a working provider with API key.
whatWeTried.4. Check models endpoint: GET /v1/models returned 1 model (profile name) but no provider backend → model exists as name but can't generate.

debuggingNarrative.rootCause: Gateway crash fix deleted HERMES_DEFAULT_MODEL and HERMES_DEFAULT_PROVIDER from Railway env vars (they had stale values that crashed the gateway). But the provisioner had no fallback — when both env vars are undefined, config.yaml gets no model/provider lines → Hermes starts but has no model configured.

fix.index.js: Add OPENROUTER_MODEL to serverEnv passed to provisioner
fix.provisioner.js: Change default model from 'serverEnv.HERMES_DEFAULT_MODEL' (could be undefined) to 'serverEnv.HERMES_DEFAULT_MODEL || serverEnv.OPENROUTER_MODEL || "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash"'. Change default provider from 'serverEnv.HERMES_DEFAULT_PROVIDER' (could be undefined) to 'serverEnv.HERMES_DEFAULT_PROVIDER || "openrouter"'

debuggingNarrative.verification: Session 200, models list 200 (1 model), message 200 with actual AI response and token counts (11526 in / 35 out tokens).
remainingRisks: Hardcoded 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash' fallback may not exist on OpenRouter yet — will return error if OPENROUTER_MODEL env var also unset.
OPENROUTER_API_KEY inherited from process.env by gateway subprocess — if this fails, Hermes can't call the API.
